key_highlights: Strong US jobs data (172,000 jobs added in May) fuels Fed rate hike expectations, sending the dollar soaring and pushing USD/JPY through 160.

Tech and AI stocks face a sharp selloff as interest rate hike fears and profit-taking hit the sector, with Meta sinking 6% and the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posting their biggest percentage drops since 2025.
Geopolitical tensions remain elevated: US-Iran war approaches 100 days, oil prices fall on de-escalation hopes, and container rates spike 109% since the war started.

AI industry faces a regulatory and public sentiment crossroads: elite celebrations in Washington contrast with public backlash against data centers and chatbots, while Trump proposes US equity stakes in AI companies.
Bitcoin plunges to a two-year low below $60K, driven by relentless selling and a Strategy sale, while crypto markets show broad weakness.
SpaceX IPO is reportedly two times oversubscribed, with a massive $920 million/month Google cloud-computing deal, signaling strong investor appetite for space and AI infrastructure.
ECB and other central banks signal further tightening, with ECB stepping up as G7's lead hawk, adding to global monetary policy headwinds.
Energy markets show mixed signals: natural gas prices retreat on abundant US supplies, jet fuel costs rise (IATA warning), and LPG prices hiked in India.
Corporate earnings and M&A activity remain active: Paramount open to selling kids channels for Warner deal, Raizen inks $13 billion creditor deal, and Frasers weighs £500 million bid.
Cathie Wood's ARK buys Amazon-backed X-Energy dip, while veteran analysts double Micron price targets, highlighting selective opportunities in tech.

sectors.trend: Sharp selloff amid rate hike fears and profit-taking; AI optimism is bifurcated with SaaS stocks rallying on AI optimism but broader tech under pressure. Meta drops 6% on stock sale report; Nvidia acquires Kumo AI for $400M; Palantir partners with Google Cloud for Gemini AI tools. Trump proposes US equity stakes in AI companies.
sectors.implications: Investors should differentiate between high-quality AI plays with real enterprise backlogs and speculative names. The selloff may present buying opportunities for long-term AI infrastructure beneficiaries (e.g., Google, Nvidia, Palantir). However, regulatory and public sentiment risks are rising.

sectors.trend: Dollar strength and rate hike expectations boost financials, but Fed's Barr warns of risks from looser Wall Street rules. ECB hikes rates, adding to global tightening. Financial pros piling into IWM (small caps) as rotation play.
sectors.implications: Banks and financial institutions may benefit from higher rates, but regulatory risks and potential credit stress from higher rates warrant caution. Small caps (IWM) could see inflows if rate hikes pause.

sectors.trend: Oil prices fall on hopes of US-Iran de-escalation; natural gas retreats on abundant US supplies; jet fuel costs rise (IATA warning); LPG prices hiked in India. Container rates spike 109% since Iran war started.
sectors.implications: Energy markets are volatile with geopolitical risks. Investors should monitor Iran war developments closely. Container rate spikes signal supply chain stress, benefiting shipping stocks but hurting consumer goods.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin plunges to two-year low below $60K, with relentless selling and a Strategy sale. Zcash drops 38% on critical vulnerability. Coinbase trending lower. Tom Lee's BitMine prices preferred shares for Ethereum strategy.
sectors.implications: Crypto markets are in a severe bear phase. Avoid speculative crypto plays; focus on established infrastructure (e.g., Coinbase) only if long-term conviction. The selloff may create entry points for patient investors.

sectors.trend: IATA warns of air transport stagflation and rising jet fuel costs. JetBlue CEO discusses potential consolidation. Delta focuses on premium travel demand. Container rates spike due to Iran war.
sectors.implications: Airlines face margin pressure from fuel costs, but premium travel demand remains strong. Consolidation could improve pricing power. Shipping rates spike may benefit logistics companies but hurt global trade.

sectors.trend: AxoGen offers entry after bullish rebound. Eli Lilly predicted to reach $1,200 in 2026. Flesh-eating screwworm returns to US, threatening cattle herd.
sectors.implications: Healthcare remains a defensive play with selective growth opportunities. Eli Lilly's obesity/diabetes pipeline is a strong long-term bet. Animal health stocks may see demand from screwworm threat.

sectors.trend: SpaceX IPO is two times oversubscribed with massive Google cloud deal. X-Energy (Amazon-backed) dips, Cathie Wood buys. Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality.
sectors.implications: Space and nuclear energy are emerging themes with strong government and corporate backing. SpaceX IPO is a must-watch; X-Energy dip may be a buying opportunity for long-term nuclear energy exposure.

key_themes: Rate hike fears dominate: Strong US jobs data fuels Fed tightening expectations, dollar surges, and risk assets sell off.
AI bubble concerns: Public sentiment sours on data centers and chatbots, while elite celebrate in Washington. Regulatory scrutiny and potential government equity stakes add uncertainty.
Geopolitical instability: US-Iran war, US sanctions on Cuba, Pakistan-Iran peace talks, and Bolivia unrest create macro risks.
Crypto winter deepens: Bitcoin at two-year low, Zcash vulnerability, and selling pressure from Strategy.
Supply chain stress: Container rates spike 109% since Iran war started, impacting global trade and inflation.
Central bank divergence: ECB hikes, Fed signals more hikes, BOJ under pressure as yen weakens through 160.
M&A and consolidation: Paramount open to selling kids channels, Frasers weighs bid, JetBlue eyes consolidation.
Energy transition: Nuclear reactor test, X-Energy investment, and AI data center power demand drive interest in alternative energy.
Consumer strain: LPG price hike in India, gas prices dip 17 cents nationally, but inflation squeezes consumers.
Space race: SpaceX IPO and Google deal highlight commercial space opportunities.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor floating-rate instruments; increase cash allocation; hedge with interest rate derivatives.
Diversify geographically; increase exposure to defense and energy stocks; hedge with gold or commodities; avoid direct exposure to conflict zones.
Focus on AI companies with strong governance and diversified revenue; avoid pure-play AI hype stocks; monitor regulatory developments closely.
Avoid leveraged crypto positions; reduce exposure to crypto-exposed stocks (e.g., Coinbase, MicroStrategy); consider short-term hedges via options.
Invest in logistics and shipping stocks; hedge with commodity futures; favor companies with pricing power; reduce exposure to consumer discretionary.
Rotate from high-growth tech to value and defensive sectors; use stop-losses on tech positions; consider buying the dip in high-quality names after further weakness.
Hedge foreign currency exposure; favor dollar-denominated assets; avoid emerging market currencies with weak fundamentals (e.g., Indonesia, Bolivia).

opportunities.recommendation: Buy the dip in high-quality AI infrastructure stocks (e.g., Nvidia, Google, Palantir) on the tech selloff.
opportunities.rationale: Long-term AI demand remains strong despite short-term rate hike fears. Nvidia's acquisition of Kumo AI and Palantir's Google Cloud partnership underscore continued investment. The selloff provides a better entry point.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate positions in SpaceX IPO when available, given strong oversubscription and Google's massive cloud deal.
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX is a leader in space and satellite internet with a clear revenue path. The Google deal validates its compute capabilities. IPO demand signals strong institutional interest.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in nuclear energy and small modular reactor plays (e.g., X-Energy dip) for long-term energy transition exposure.
opportunities.rationale: Small modular nuclear reactor reaching criticality is a milestone. AI data center power demand will drive interest in reliable, clean energy. Cathie Wood's ARK buying the dip signals confidence.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider long positions in Eli Lilly (LLY) on any pullback, targeting $1,200 by year-end 2026.
opportunities.rationale: Eli Lilly's obesity/diabetes pipeline is a multi-billion dollar opportunity. The stock has strong fundamentals and analyst support. Healthcare is defensive in a rate hike environment.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy IWM (small-cap ETF) as a rotation play if rate hikes pause, given financial pros piling in.
opportunities.rationale: Small caps are undervalued relative to large caps and could benefit from a pause in rate hikes. The rotation theme is supported by institutional inflows.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation to 20-30% of portfolio to hedge against further rate hike shocks and market volatility.
defensive_moves.rationale: Strong jobs data and hawkish Fed/ECB signals suggest more tightening ahead. Cash provides optionality to buy dips and reduces portfolio drawdown risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-growth tech and crypto-exposed stocks; rotate into defensive sectors (healthcare, utilities, consumer staples).
defensive_moves.rationale: Tech and crypto are under severe pressure from rate hikes and sentiment shifts. Defensive sectors offer stability and dividends. Eli Lilly and utility stocks are good candidates.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against dollar strength by reducing exposure to emerging market currencies and assets.
defensive_moves.rationale: Dollar is soaring on rate hike expectations, pressuring EM currencies (e.g., Indonesia, Bolivia). Avoid EM debt and equities until dollar stabilizes.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Use put options or inverse ETFs to hedge against further tech selloff, especially in AI and SaaS stocks.
defensive_moves.rationale: Tech valuations are vulnerable to rate hikes and regulatory risks. Options hedging can limit downside while maintaining upside exposure.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to oil and gas stocks given falling oil prices on de-escalation hopes; rotate into renewable energy.
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil prices are falling as US-Iran war de-escalation hopes rise. Renewable energy and nuclear are better long-term plays given AI data center demand.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish with high volatility. Rate hike fears will continue to pressure risk assets, especially tech and crypto. The dollar will remain strong, hurting EM and commodities. Geopolitical risks (Iran, Bolivia) add uncertainty. Investors should focus on defensive sectors and cash. A potential pause in rate hikes could trigger a relief rally, but the trend is downward until inflation data softens.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic. AI and space infrastructure remain secular growth themes. Once rate hikes peak, tech and crypto could recover strongly. Nuclear energy and healthcare offer long-term value. Geopolitical resolution (Iran) could boost global trade and reduce supply chain stress. However, structural inflation and regulatory risks (AI, crypto) will cap upside.
key_catalysts: Fed interest rate decision in June/July 2026
SpaceX IPO pricing and trading debut
AI regulatory framework proposals (US equity stakes, EU AI Act)
Bitcoin and crypto market stabilization or further decline
ECB and BOJ policy moves (yen through 160)
Q2 2026 earnings season (tech and AI revenue growth)
US presidential election campaign (Trump's AI equity proposal)
Container rate trends and global trade data
watch_list: US 10-year Treasury yield (above 5% signals further stress)
DXY index (above 110 signals EM crisis risk)
Bitcoin price (support at $50K; break below could trigger cascading liquidations)
Oil price (WTI below $70 signals recession fears)
IATA air transport data (fuel costs, passenger demand)
Bolivia lithium production and political stability
